title: A Giant Space Rock Hit the North Sea and Made a Huge Wave
summary: Scientists have solved a big mystery about a hole under the North Sea called Silverpit Crater. They found out that a huge asteroid or comet crashed there about 45 million years ago. This crash made a giant tsunami wave as tall as a 30-story building. The crater is hidden deep under the sea, and for years, people argued about how it formed. Some thought it was from an asteroid, while others said it was from salt or volcanoes moving underground. Now, new pictures and rock samples show it was definitely an asteroid impact. The scientists used special tools to look at the rocks and make computer models. This discovery helps us learn more about Earth's history and how space rocks can change our planet.

background_read: The Silverpit Crater is a big hole under the North Sea that scientists have been studying for years. It was first found in 2002, and people argued about how it formed. Some thought an asteroid made it, while others said salt or volcanoes caused it. Now, new research shows it was an asteroid impact that also created a huge tsunami. This helps us understand how space rocks can affect Earth and why it's important to study our planet's past.
Article_Structure: WHO: Dr. Uisdean Nicholson and a team of scientists
WHAT: They found that an asteroid hit the North Sea and made a crater and tsunami
WHERE: Under the North Sea, off the coast of Yorkshire
WHY: To solve a mystery and learn about Earth's history

perspectives.description: Some scientists believed the crater was made by an asteroid hitting Earth.
Other scientists thought salt movement or volcanoes caused the crater instead.
